steps.title: Turn the words into two rows
steps.content: Use a quick table check. If $x=0$, then $y$ is $8$ more than twice $0$, so $y=8$. If $x=1$, then $y$ is $8$ more than twice $1$, so $y=10$.

steps.title: Fit the line
steps.content: Run $y_1 \sim mx_1+b$ in Desmos. The regression gives $m=2$ and $b=8$, so the equation is $y=2x+8$.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content: Choice A and choice C have slope $10$, not $2$. Choice D has slope $8$ and intercept $2$, which swaps the two numbers from the phrase. Choice B, $y=2x+8$, matches the regression and the wording. Choice B is correct.
